Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Trial By Jury; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Alexander Xue
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF TRIAL BY JURY

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. Plaintiff's proof of service was not timely filed. (See Rule of Court 3.1300, subd. (c) ["Proof of service of the moving papers must be filed no later than five court days before the time appointed for the hearing."].) The motion should be renoticed for hearing in Department 206, which handles jury trial waiver issues and is generally responsible for matters related to trial setting. Defendant's proposed order, which is disputed, is submitted to the court for review along with comments received from plaintiff. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances are made in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Alexander Xue's demurrer to defendant Doppio Zero SF, LLP's answer to the complaint is sustained as to the first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses without leave to amend and overruled as to all other affirmative defenses. Because no notice was given ending the tolling period for filing the demurrer, the demurrer is not untimely filed. CCP 431.40 is inapplicable because the defendant did not file a response to the complaint in a "form prescribed by the Judicial Council." The first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses are not true affirmative defenses. All the other affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged and are not uncertain per the norms of pleading affirmative defenses in this and other counties. If the plaintiff wishes any further information about the affirmative defenses for which his demurrer is overruled, he may serve appropriate discovery. If the plaintiff believes that there is no merit to any of those affirmative defenses, he may file a motion for summary adjudication.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: S. Kane, Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances are made in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Alexander Xue's motion to strike portions of defendant Doppio Zero SF, LLP's answer to the complaint is granted as to the first two sentences in the second paragraph on page 1 of the answer, off calendar as to the first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses, and denied as to all other affirmative defenses. Because no notice was given ending the tolling period for filing the motion, the motion is not untimely filed. CCP 431.40 is inapplicable because the defendant did not file a response to the complaint in a "form prescribed by the Judicial Council." The first, eighth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth, twenty-first and twenty-second affirmative defenses have been dealt with in the ruling on plaintiff's demurrer to the answer. All the other affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged and are not uncertain per the norms of pleading affirmative defenses in this and other counties. If the plaintiff wishes any further information about the affirmative defenses for which his motion to strike is denied, he may serve appropriate discovery. If the plaintiff believes that there is no merit to any of those affirmative defenses, he may file a motion for summary adjudication.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: S. Kane, Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT DOPPIO ZERO SF, LP's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed demurrer of defendant Doppio Zero SF, LP is overruled.

Plaintiff Alexander Xue alleges that he ate a meal at Doppio Zero and was charged an undisclosed fee on his final bill. In demurrer to the first and second causes of action for violations of the Unfair Competition Law, Doppio Zero's argument depends on its claim that the fee was disclosed. Xue pleads that the fee was not disclosed, a fact the court must accept as true for purposes of this pleadings motion.

The third cause of action pleads a claim under the Consumers Legal Remedy Act. Doppio Zero contends that Xue cannot receive damages, much less damages or disgorgement for fees paid by other customers, because he does not plead that he sent a cure notice to Doppio Zero before filing suit. But Xue seeks injunctive relief as well as damages under this cause of action. Because a demurrer lies only to an entire cause of action and not elements of relief, this argument is an insufficient basis to sustain the demurrer.

Doppio Zero's demurrer was previously set for hearing on July 18, 2025. The court continued it because Xue provided a courtesy copy of a purported opposition brief to the court but neither filed it nor served it on Doppio Zero. The court has not read or relied on the purported opposition, and cautions Xue that the court cannot consider opposition papers that are not filed. Moreover, all materials provided to the court must be served on the opposing party. The court orders Xue not to deliver courtesy copies to the court of material he has not served.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Atty. Jordanna Thigpen, Esq. of Thigpen Legal, P.C. (tel: 310-210-7491) for the Defendant.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
